Title: Hillbilly Elegy
Author: J.D. Vance
Notes: Read critically. Oversimplifies structural poverty. Useful for knowing the argument to counter.

Title: Thurgood Marshall: American Revolutionary
Author: Juan Williams
Notes: Essential. Annotated heavily. Inspiration for civil rights litigation strategy.

Title: Just Mercy
Author: Bryan Stevenson
Notes: Third reread. Still the clearest moral map I have for this work.

Title: Poverty and the Law
Author: Peter Edelman
Notes: Dense but essential for housing class action framing.

Title: The House on Mango Street
Author: Sandra Cisneros
Notes: Quiet and devastating. Read for a break from case files.

Title: Appalachian Quilts: Heritage Patterns and Stories
Author: Cuesta Benberry
Notes: Primary reference for Stoneridge Museum commission research.

Title: The New Jim Crow
Author: Michelle Alexander
Notes: Core reading. Referenced in Franklin County case research notes.

Title: Where the Crawdads Sing
Author: Delia Owens
Notes: Good escapism on a hard week.

Title: Braiding Sweetgrass
Author: Robin Wall Kimmerer
Notes: Bought for Jackie. Read it twice myself.

Title: The Color of Law
Author: Richard Rothstein
Notes: Directly cited in Franklin County housing case briefs.

Title: Fair Housing Act Cases and Materials
Author: Robert G. Schwemm
Notes: Working through for disparate impact case law updates.

Title: Educated
Author: Tara Westover
Notes: Resonant. Rural isolation as both protection and prison.
